description: Single-note guitar samples per (string, fret) — open + selected fretted positions. WAV files are fetched on demand from GuitarSet via fetch.sh; only the E2_open.wav fixture is committed as a deterministic offline-test smoke target.

entries.context: Standard tuning's lowest open string (82.41 Hz). Tonic of E minor — the workhorse key of blues and rock guitar; also the bass anchor for every E-major open chord.
entries.attribution: Synthesised in-tree fixture, dedicated CC0 1.0.
entries.fixture: synthesised E2 sine envelope, 2 s, 44.1 kHz mono 16-bit

entries.context: Fret 1 on the E2 string — raises the open string by 1 semitone(s).
entries.attribution: GuitarSet (Xi, Bittner, Pauwels, Ye, Bello — ISMIR 2018), licensed BSD-3-Clause.

entries.context: Fret 3 on the E2 string — raises the open string by 3 semitone(s).
entries.attribution: GuitarSet (Xi, Bittner, Pauwels, Ye, Bello — ISMIR 2018), licensed BSD-3-Clause.

entries.context: Standard tuning A. Open root for the canonical 'cowboy chord' A major and tonic of A minor; the string the entire 'A-shape' barre-chord family is voiced over.
entries.attribution: GuitarSet (Xi, Bittner, Pauwels, Ye, Bello — ISMIR 2018), licensed BSD-3-Clause.

entries.context: Standard tuning D. Mid-range bass string; the bass note of every D-major-key folk progression and the drone in DADGAD-derived alternative tunings.
entries.attribution: GuitarSet (Xi, Bittner, Pauwels, Ye, Bello — ISMIR 2018), licensed BSD-3-Clause.

entries.context: Standard tuning G. The single most-played open root in beginner classical and folk fingerstyle repertoire; G major dominates the Mutopia catalogue.

entries.context: Standard tuning B. The string whose interval to the next-up E breaks the all-fourths pattern (it's a major third) — historical compromise that makes open-position chord shapes playable.
entries.attribution: GuitarSet (Xi, Bittner, Pauwels, Ye, Bello — ISMIR 2018), licensed BSD-3-Clause.

entries.context: Standard tuning's high E (329.63 Hz). Top voice of the open chord. The 12th-fret natural harmonic of the low E lands at this exact pitch — a useful self-check for octave doubling in physical models.
entries.attribution: GuitarSet (Xi, Bittner, Pauwels, Ye, Bello — ISMIR 2018), licensed BSD-3-Clause.
